revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Akamonue Kofun]][[ja:赤門上古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site|image=File:Akamon-kohun-003.jpg|location={{ill|Hamakita Ward|qid=Q1203662|1=Hamakita-ku}}|coordinates={{Coord|34.78805556|N|137.75277778|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|architectural_styles={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|qid=Q11268718|1=Zenpōkōenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}}}}
[[File:Akamon-kohun-003.jpg|right|thumb|300px|Akamonue kofun]] {{nihongo|'''Akamon-ue Kofun'''|赤門上古墳||qq=}} is a keyhole-shaped ''[[kofun]]'' [[Kofun]] located in the Uchino district of {{ill|Hamana-ku, Hamamatsu|en|Hamana-ku, Hamamatsu|qid=Q117077169|lt=Hamana-ku|1=Hamana-ku, Hamamatsu}}, [[:en:Hamamatsu, Shizuoka|Hamamatsu]], {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|en|Shizuoka Prefecture|qid=Q131320|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}} {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. It is protected by the prefectural government as a national historic site. Located on the eastern edge of the Mikatahara plains and near the [[Tenryū River]], the Akamonue Kofun is only one of several ''kofun'' in the same district. It takes its name from the red gate of a nearby Buddhist temple, which was built at a much later date. The name or Rank of the person buried in the tomb, which dates from the latter half of the 4th century is unknown. The ''kofun'' was [[:en:Excavation (archaeology)|excavated]] in the summer of 1961 by a team of students from the Shizuoka Prefectural Hamana High School under the direction of the Hamamatsu City Cultural Affairs Department. The team soon found evidence that the ''kofun'' had been plundered at some unknown time in the past, and that a portion had been used as an {{ill|Air raid shelter|en|Air raid shelter|qid=Q389959|lt=Air raid shelter|1=Air raid shelter}} in {{ill|Pacific War|en|Pacific War|qid=Q184425|lt=Pacific War|1=Pacific War}}. The total length of the ''kofun'' is 56.3 meters. The round portion has a circumference of 36.2 meters and a height of 4.9 meters. The rectangular portion had a length of 14.7 meters and height of 1.15 meters. Within the burial chamber, a 5.58 meter long ''[[hinoki]]'' {{ill|Sarcophagus|en|Sarcophagus|qid=Q48634|lt=Sarcophagus|1=Sarcophagus}} containing numerous funerary objects was discovered. Artifacts included bits of armor and weapons, and most notably, an ancient bronze mirror. [[Image:Sankakubuchishinjukyo.jpg|right|250px|Bronze Mirror recovered from Akamonue Kofun]] ==References== {{reflist|qq=}} ==External links== *[https://web.archive.org/web/20090930112312/http://www.city.shizuoka.jp/deps/bunkazai/bunkazai_tyousa_shizuhatakohun_index.htm Shizuoka City site] {{coord|34|47|17|N|137|45|10|E|region:JP_type:edu|display=title|qq=}}
{{Kofun navbar|qq=}}
